brief.title: aion-awl emitter split: emitter.rs (2043 lines) into an emitter/ folder module of real named children each <=500 lines, ZERO behavior change, mechanically fenced scope. PURE SPLIT — emitter.rs carries NO allow block and NO lint debt (base clippy green), so there is nothing to purge (mirrors the 001a checker split).
brief.objective: Split crates/aion-awl/src/emitter.rs (2043 lines) into a folder module crates/aion-awl/src/emitter/ whose mod.rs contains ONLY module declarations and re-exports, with every child file at or under 500 lines — all at ZERO behavior cost. This is a PURE MOVE: authoring measurement confirmed emitter.rs has NO lint-bypass block and NO lint debt (base `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` is green with the file exactly as-is), so there is NOTHING to purge and NO lint-motivated logic edit is warranted. The crate root already declares `mod emitter;` (lib.rs line 8), which resolves to emitter/mod.rs automatically — lib.rs therefore needs NO edit and MUST NOT be edited. The only edits permitted are the mechanical relocation and the `pub(super)`/`pub(crate)` visibility widenings the move forces (e.g. exposing the private `EmitError::new` and free helper fns across child modules). Structure guidance from the real seams (the emitter is one `Emitter<'a>` struct with a single large impl plus free helper functions): the public error type + entry point (EmitError + new/Display/Error impls + `pub fn emit` + the free opaque_ref_error); the Emitter struct + Binding + TerminatingHandler + `new` + the `emit` driver method + output primitives (line/blank/indented/indented_try/capture); module-frame emission (header/types/emit_type/definition/run); step machinery (execute/emit_steps/check_step/emit_terminating_step/emit_terminating_case/emit_flat_step/emit_step_expr/emit_attempt/step_inner); bounded-repeat loops (emit_repeat_call/render_loop_fn/emit_loop_round/loop_free_names + free loop_counter_name/child_retry_params); call/handler/binding lowering (check_no_opaque_refs/emit_handler_arm/record_binding/write_activity_value/write_call_pipeline/write_child_pipeline + free empty_step/collect_expr_refs/collect_named_ref); codec + wrapper emission (codecs/emit_external_codec/emit_codec/collect_composite_types/emit_composite_codec/builtin_codecs/activity_wrappers/signal_refs/error_mappers/child_helpers — a large unit that may split further to stay under 500 lines); and pure naming/formatting helpers (external_named_types/collect_named_refs/action/input_type_name/output_type_name + free gleam_type/codec_name/expr/parenthesized/binary_op/duration_ms/duration_expr/retry_policy/string_lit/constructor/pascal/snake/is_gleam_keyword/ident/wrap_doc/is_builtin_type/collect_composite_type). Multiple `impl Emitter<'a>` blocks across child modules of one crate are legal Rust and the intended technique (001a split the large Ctx impl across checker/{context,declarations,expressions,steps,types,idents}.rs); free helpers and the private `EmitError::new` take `pub(super)` visibility (an internal widening, not a public-API/signature change). Choose child-module names from the actual responsibilities you find (e.g. emitter/error.rs, emitter/context.rs, emitter/module_frame.rs, emitter/steps.rs, emitter/loops.rs, emitter/calls.rs, emitter/codecs.rs, emitter/helpers.rs) — mechanically named files (part_00.rs, chunk_1.rs, body.rs holding everything) and include!() textual splicing are REJECTED outright. CRITICAL re-export (R2): lib.rs re-exports `pub use emitter::{EmitError, emit};` (line 20), so emitter/mod.rs MUST re-export both EmitError and emit to keep the crate-root path resolving byte-identically. Authoring verified there is NO intra-crate consumer of a private emitter item (the only `emitter::` occurrence in the workspace is lib.rs line 20 — no duration_text-style pub(crate) trap), and emitter.rs imports from `crate::` (ast re-exports + Span) NOT from `crate::parser::`, so the in-flight 001c parser split cannot affect it.
brief.context:
Fourth of five single-file sub-briefs decomposed from AWL0-REFAC-001 (see AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md Wave 0 and BRIEF-CRAFT.md entry 10), stamped from the 001a/001b/001c templates. 001a (checker split) run a96973cb merged adb6e4cf; 001b (lib.rs split + 4-attr purge) run 3d201c36 merged 80426594; 001c (parser split) preceded this brief. crates/aion-awl/src/emitter.rs is 2043 lines, over the 500-line limit and the largest file in the crate. THIS BRIEF IS A PURE SPLIT, NOT A PURGE. The AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN Wave 0 entry for 001d is only `emitter.rs (2043) -> emitter/` (no allow-block clause), and the plan's pre-existing-bypass inventory does NOT list emitter.rs. Authoring measured this on base 81b0fa06: emitter.rs line 1 is `use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};` (NOT an allow block); grep for bypass attributes over emitter.rs returns ZERO matches; `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` is GREEN on the UNMODIFIED file; there are ZERO `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` sites (only allowed `.unwrap_or*` combinators); the largest function (render_loop_fn ~94 lines) is under the too_many_lines threshold of 100 so NO function triggers it; and EmitError + its public fields already carry real doc comments. So emitter.rs MIRRORS checker.rs (001a: `No pre-existing attrs`), and R3 is `introduce no bypass; there is no debt to purge`, NOT the purge-by-doing-the-work shape of 001b/001c. emitter.rs is DELETED and replaced by the folder (lib.rs's `mod emitter;` line 8 resolves it), so 001d mirrors 001a's shape, including 001a/001c's path-exclude scope fence.

INTENTION: Fourth stamp of the decomposed pipe-cleaner sequence (001a..e). The monolithic AWL0-REFAC-001 failed twice — once by fraud (include! splices + crate-wide allow), once by an honest rollback after cascading errors mid-split of four files at once. This sub-brief is sized to the fix-cycle envelope (one 2043-line file, a pure mechanical move); every non-negotiable is a mechanical gate (three GREEN ON BASE, one — split-complete — RED ON BASE because it is the work); the scope fence makes wandering impossible; and the full measured evidence is embedded below so the developer neither hunts for phantom purge work nor introduces gratuitous edits under cover of the split.

CONSTRAINTS:
- CN1: Canonical docs (AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md, AWL-0-SPEC-DRAFT.md, AWL-BC-DESIGN-DRAFT.md, AWL-UX.md, examples/) are the reading list; the plan's Wave 0 spec for this sub-brief is the CONTRACT — implement it verbatim. Discovered ambiguities are findings to report, never license to improvise.
- CN2: Coding standards (CLAUDE.md): no #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses, thiserror in libraries, no unwrap()/expect()/panic!() in library code, files <=500 lines, mod.rs = declarations/re-exports only, no silent failures.
- CN3: Gate argv, run EXACTLY in order before reporting: mechanical gates (no-bypasses-scoped, lints-pinned, scope-fence, split-complete) -> cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli.
- CN4: Round-trip property is sacred: parse . print = id and print . parse . print = print, byte-level, for every fixture. The emitter's own regression invariant is stronger: the emitted Gleam must stay byte-identical to the three committed .gleam.golden files. The scope fence makes perturbing either impossible without failing a gate.
- CN5: Review lenses: the standard adversarial set PLUS a spec-fidelity lens — one reviewer reads the named plan section and the committed brief and checks the implementation clause by clause against R1-R4, including confirming R3 required NO purge work (emitter.rs had no debt) and that no gratuitous logic edit slipped in.
- CN6: Bar reminders: stage explicit paths (never `git add -A`); inspect with `git diff --no-ext-diff`; NEVER trust the workflow's own green — the operator re-runs the gates by hand before merge.

The full R1-R4 contract with acceptance criteria is committed at docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001d-input.json — read it in the workspace.
pointers: docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001a-input.json (the pilot's contract — emitter.rs is deleted+foldered exactly like checker.rs was, and like checker.rs it has NO pre-existing attrs, so 001a is the closest template)
docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001c-input.json (the third stamp — same folder-split + scope-fence mechanics, but note 001c had a real allow-block purge that 001d does NOT)
docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md (Wave 0 — pipe-cleaner; the AWL0-REFAC-001d entry, which lists NO allow-block clause for emitter.rs)
docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/BRIEF-CRAFT.md (why the gates are shaped this way; entries 8-12, principle 10 MEASURE-before-mandating, and the run a96973cb / 3d201c36 validation notes)
crates/aion-awl/src/emitter.rs (the 2043-line file to split; DELETE it, replaced by emitter/ — it has no bypass block and no lint debt)
crates/aion-awl/src/checker/ and crates/aion-awl/src/lexer/ (the two landed folder-module layouts to mirror — private folder module, mod.rs = declarations/re-exports only)

crates/aion-awl/src/lib.rs (crate root — DO NOT EDIT; `mod emitter;` line 8 already resolves the folder; `pub use emitter::{EmitError, emit};` line 20 must keep resolving via emitter/mod.rs re-export)
crates/aion-cli/src/awl.rs (DO NOT EDIT; line 164 `aion_awl::emit(&document)` + `error.span`/`error.message` must keep resolving through the crate root)

crates/aion-awl/tests/emitter.rs + crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/{hello,research_report,bounded_cycle}.gleam.golden (DO NOT EDIT; assert_golden pins the emitted Gleam byte-for-byte — this is the regression invariant)
scope_in: crates/aion-awl/src/emitter.rs (delete, replaced by the folder module)
crates/aion-awl/src/emitter/ (create: mod.rs + responsibility-named children, each <=500 lines; mod.rs = declarations/re-exports only, including the `pub use ...::{EmitError, emit};` re-export)
scope_out: REAL NAMED MODULES ONLY: every child file is a genuine `mod` whose name describes its responsibility. include!() textual splicing is FORBIDDEN. Mechanically-named files (part_00.rs, chunk_*.rs, body.rs-holding-everything) are REJECTED regardless of line counts. Dispatch 1 of the parent brief was rejected for include!("parts/part_00.rs") splices behind 2-line mod.rs files.

PURE MOVE, NO GRATUITOUS EDITS: emitter.rs has no bypass block and no lint debt (measurement-confirmed), so add NO #![allow]/#[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] anywhere in emitter/, make NO lint-motivated logic edit (no function decomposition, no unwrap/expect rewrite — there were none, no doc churn), and keep crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml byte-identical — dispatch 2 of the parent brief was rejected for replacing `[lints] workspace = true` with a private allow-list. Both escape hatches are gated mechanically.

lib.rs MUST NOT be edited: `mod emitter;` (line 8) already resolves the folder module; `pub use emitter::{EmitError, emit};` (line 20) is unchanged. If you believe an edit outside the fence is required, STOP and report it as a finding instead of making it.

aion-cli MUST NOT be edited: it imports `aion_awl::emit` and `aion_awl::EmitError` (crates/aion-cli/src/awl.rs line 164) through the crate root. Keep those resolving by re-exporting EmitError and emit from emitter/mod.rs — the scope-fence gate fails on any aion-cli edit.

ZERO behavior change: the split is a pure move; the only edits permitted are the mechanical relocation and the pub(super)/pub(crate) visibility widenings it forces. No diagnostic-text change, no span change, no AST-shape change, no verdict change, and NO change to the byte-for-byte emitted Gleam (the three .gleam.golden files stay byte-identical and pass unmodified). Round-trip property (CN4) untouched.
Existing tests, fixtures, goldens, and every non-emitter file UNMODIFIED — enforced by the scope-fence gate.

Pre-existing bypass attributes ELSEWHERE in the crate (ast.rs missing_docs, printer.rs missing_docs, three test files; and until 001c lands, parser.rs:1) are OUT OF SCOPE — they belong to sub-briefs 001c/001e. Leave them exactly as they are; the gates here are scoped so they do not fire on them, and the scope fence forbids touching them.
SHALL NOT touch any crate other than aion-awl, nor anything in aion-awl outside src/emitter*.
acceptance: no-bypasses-scoped gate (GREEN on base): grep over the landed-clean paths (checker, lib.rs, lexer) plus parser/ and emitter/ once present returns no allow/expect/ignore/include! matches. This gate scans only EXISTING paths (it does not name the deleted checker.rs, the not-yet-split parser.rs, or emitter.rs the file), so it is reliable and green on base — even while parser.rs still carries its line-1 block until 001c lands — and goes red on any real bypass under a scanned path.
lints-pinned gate (GREEN on base): crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml has exactly one [lints] section, containing workspace = true.
scope-fence gate: git diff versus the pinned base SHA, excluding crates/aion-awl/src/emitter.rs and crates/aion-awl/src/emitter, is empty.
split-complete gate (RED on base by design): emitter.rs gone AND emitter/mod.rs present AND every emitter/*.rs at or under 500 lines AND zero bypass attributes under emitter/.

cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli, all clean. (The full workspace suite is run by the operator at merge review while tasks #243/#244 are open.)

Hand review: emitter/mod.rs is declarations + re-exports only (including the `pub use ...::{EmitError, emit};` re-export); child names are honest and each <=500 lines; NO bypass exists under emitter/ and NO gratuitous logic edit was made (the diff is the move plus visibility widenings only); `pub use emitter::{EmitError, emit};` byte-compatible; the three .gleam.golden files byte-identical and passing; printer.rs/lib.rs/ast.rs/checker/lexer/parser/aion-cli untouched.
brief.notes:
DISPATCH HISTORY (read this first): the parent brief AWL0-REFAC-001 was dispatched twice and REJECTED twice at operator review, then decomposed into five single-file sub-briefs. Dispatch 1 faked the split with include!("parts/part_00.rs") textual splices behind 2-line mod.rs files plus a crate-wide #![allow(...)]. Dispatch 2 rolled the split back after cascading errors and instead replaced aion-awl's Cargo.toml `[lints] workspace = true` with a private allow-list to silence clippy. BOTH escape hatches are now mechanical gates that run before anything expensive; do the real work. The pilots then landed FIRST-PASS clean: 001a (checker split) run a96973cb merged adb6e4cf, 001b (lib.rs split + 4-attr purge) run 3d201c36 merged 80426594 (8/8 gates, 4/4 lenses, zero deviations each); 001c (parser split) preceded this brief. This brief is stamped from them. Honest reporting was noted favourably: if something genuinely cannot be done within the boundaries, say so plainly in your report rather than working around a gate.

THIS IS A PURE SPLIT (measure-before-mandating, BRIEF-CRAFT principle 10): unlike 001b (four vestigial attrs deleted) and 001c (a line-1 block hiding 32 real clippy errors), emitter.rs on base carries NO bypass block and NO lint debt. It mirrors 001a (checker: `No pre-existing attrs`). Do NOT hunt for purge work that does not exist, and do NOT introduce gratuitous logic edits under cover of the split — the reviewer treats any non-mechanical edit (a decomposed function, a rewritten unwrap that was never there, doc churn) as a regression risk to justify.

TECHNIQUE: emitter.rs is DELETED and replaced by a private emitter/ folder module — lib.rs already declares `mod emitter;` (line 8) which resolves emitter/mod.rs automatically, so you need NO lib.rs edit and the scope-fence gate fails if you make one. Mirror the two landed layouts (checker/, lexer/): a folder whose mod.rs is `mod <child>;` declarations + `pub use` re-exports ONLY, with children named for responsibility, each <=500 lines. The file is one `Emitter<'a>` struct with a single large impl (lines ~91-1656) plus free helper functions (lines ~1658-2043). Split the impl across child modules with multiple `impl Emitter<'a>` blocks (multiple impl blocks for one type across files of one crate is legal Rust and the intended approach — 001a did exactly this for the 530-line Ctx impl). Free helper functions and the private `EmitError::new` associated fn, once referenced from more than one child module, need `pub(super)` (or `pub(crate)`) visibility — an internal widening, not a public-API/signature change. Natural seams: error.rs (EmitError + new/Display/Error + `pub fn emit` + free opaque_ref_error); context.rs (Emitter struct + Binding + TerminatingHandler + new + the emit driver method + output primitives line/blank/indented/indented_try/capture); module_frame.rs (header/types/emit_type/definition/run); steps.rs (execute/emit_steps/check_step/emit_terminating_step/emit_terminating_case/emit_flat_step/emit_step_expr/emit_attempt/step_inner); loops.rs (emit_repeat_call/render_loop_fn/emit_loop_round/loop_free_names + free loop_counter_name/child_retry_params); calls.rs (check_no_opaque_refs/emit_handler_arm/record_binding/write_activity_value/write_call_pipeline/write_child_pipeline + free empty_step/collect_expr_refs/collect_named_ref); codecs.rs (codecs/emit_external_codec/emit_codec/collect_composite_types/emit_composite_codec/builtin_codecs/activity_wrappers/signal_refs/error_mappers/child_helpers — the largest concern, may split into two to stay under 500); helpers.rs (external_named_types/collect_named_refs/action/input_type_name/output_type_name + free gleam_type/codec_name/expr/parenthesized/binary_op/duration_ms/duration_expr/retry_policy/string_lit/constructor/pascal/snake/is_gleam_keyword/ident/wrap_doc/is_builtin_type/collect_composite_type) — or an equivalent honest arrangement; each file <=500 lines. RE-EXPORT TRAP: lib.rs line 20 `pub use emitter::{EmitError, emit};` — emitter/mod.rs MUST `pub use` both so the crate root resolves byte-identically. Authoring verified there is NO intra-crate `use crate::emitter::X` consumer anywhere in the workspace (the ONLY `emitter::` occurrence is lib.rs line 20), so unlike 001c's duration_text there is NO additional pub(crate) re-export trap; and the out-of-crate consumer aion-cli (crates/aion-cli/src/awl.rs line 164) reaches `aion_awl::emit`/`aion_awl::EmitError` through the crate root, so it compiles unmodified as long as the crate-root re-export resolves. emitter.rs imports from `crate::` (ast re-exports + the lexer Span) and NOT from `crate::parser::` (authoring-verified), so the in-flight 001c parser split does not touch it.

MEASURED CLIPPY INVENTORY (authoring, on base 81b0fa068dc670ecbecb083e8efd4368c0ba69f5, running `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` against the UNMODIFIED emitter.rs — NOT with any block deleted, because there is no block): ZERO errors, ZERO warnings — the base is GREEN as-is. Breakdown of why there is nothing to purge: (a) missing_docs — 0 sites: `pub struct EmitError` and its fields `span`/`message` already carry real doc comments (lines 12-22), and every other item in the file is private (`fn`/`struct`/`enum`, not `pub`), so missing_docs does not apply. (b) clippy::unnecessary_wraps — 0 sites: NO public fn triggers it, so the STOP-on-public-rename condition does NOT fire. (c) clippy::too_many_lines — 0 sites: the largest function `render_loop_fn` is ~94 source lines (under the 100 threshold); the next largest are `codecs` (~86), `child_helpers` (~73), `check_step` (~73), `write_child_pipeline` (~71) — all under 100. The folder split does not need to touch any function body. (d) clippy::expect_used + clippy::unwrap_used — 0 sites: grep for bare `.unwrap()`/`.expect(` over emitter.rs returns NOTHING; the only unwrap-family calls are the allowed combinators `.unwrap_or_default()` (lines 411, 666), `.unwrap_or_else(...)` (lines 680, 681), and `.unwrap_or(...)` (line 902), none of which trigger the lints. There is NO #![allow] block at line 1 (line 1 is `use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};`). Consequently R3 is satisfied by NOT introducing a bypass while moving code — no doc-writing, no unwrap rewriting, no function decomposition, no signature change.
SIZING: this is the largest file (2043 lines) but the LIGHTEST work of the four splits — a pure mechanical move with zero purge and zero behavior-touching edits, closest to 001a's clean pilot (which landed first-pass in one dev round). Authoring judges it fits one dispatch (1 dev round + 3 fix cycles) comfortably: the only skill required is a careful multi-impl-block folder split with correct pub(super) visibility, and the byte-identical .gleam.golden tests catch any behavioral slip immediately. NO split of the dispatch is recommended. If the codec concern crowds a single child over 500 lines, split it into two named children (e.g. codecs.rs + wrappers.rs) rather than shrinking a function.

GATES: all mechanical gates except split-complete were verified GREEN on the base ref at authoring, so any red in those is yours; split-complete is RED on base BY DESIGN (emitter.rs is 2043 lines and exists) and turns green only when emitter.rs is gone, emitter/mod.rs exists, every emitter/*.rs is <=500 lines, and zero bypasses remain under emitter/ — that is the work. NOTE on gate 1 (no-bypasses-scoped): it guards the two LANDED splits (checker/, lexer/) and the crate root (lib.rs) unconditionally, plus parser/ and emitter/ conditionally once each EXISTS. It deliberately scans only paths that EXIST — it does NOT name the deleted checker.rs, the not-yet-split parser.rs, or emitter.rs the file — because a nonexistent path makes grep exit with an error code that the leading `!` would flip into a false green (the operator's grep is ugrep 7.5.0, on which a missing path exits 2 and the `!` flips it to false-green — proven). CRITICALLY, because parser.rs the FILE still carries its line-1 `#![allow(...)]` block until 001c lands, gate 1 must NOT scan parser.rs the file; the `[ -e crates/aion-awl/src/parser ]` test matches the DIRECTORY parser/ (which does not exist until 001c lands), so on the current base gate 1 does not scan parser at all and stays GREEN despite parser.rs's block (authoring-verified: gate 1 exit 0 on base with parser.rs's `#![allow(` present at line 1). Likewise `[ -e crates/aion-awl/src/emitter ]` matches the DIRECTORY, so emitter.rs the file is never scanned on base; once the developer creates emitter/, the directory is scanned. Gate 1 was verified to go RED on a synthetic bypass planted in emitter/ AND (still guarding the landed split) in lexer/. The scope-fence gate is pinned to a base SHA: the literal string PINNED_SHA_AT_DISPATCH in the scope-fence argv MUST be replaced by the operator with `git rev-parse main` at dispatch time (re-pin to fresh main HEAD — if 001c merges before this dispatch, main will have moved and the pin MUST be refreshed), and the operator re-verifies gates 1-3 green + gate 4 red on that base before dispatching. The test gates are scoped (aion-awl + aion-cli) and deterministic — the flaky workspace suite is deliberately NOT a gate here (tasks #243/#244: aion-package `append_run_regenerates_type_checking_gleam` and aion-server --lib both flake under load in crates this diff never touches); if a workspace test outside aion-awl/aion-cli ever runs and flakes while every in-scope gate passes and your diff touches only crates/aion-awl/src/emitter*, re-run once and report the flake honestly rather than attempting an out-of-scope fix. The operator runs the full workspace suite by hand at merge review. AUTHORING VERIFICATION (clean detached worktree on base 81b0fa068dc670ecbecb083e8efd4368c0ba69f5, PINNED_SHA substituted to that SHA): no-bypasses-scoped exit 0 (with parser.rs's line-1 block present and unscanned), lints-pinned exit 0, scope-fence exit 0 on the clean tree (and correctly exit 1 on a synthetic out-of-scope printer.rs edit); split-complete exit 1 on base (emitter.rs present); with a synthetic in-scope split simulated, scope-fence stayed 0 and split-complete flipped to 0, and gate 1 went RED on a synthetic bypass planted in emitter/ and separately in lexer/. Base `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` was verified GREEN on the unmodified file (confirming R3 has no purge work).

gates.name: no-bypasses-scoped
argv: ps="crates/aion-awl/src/checker crates/aion-awl/src/lib.rs crates/aion-awl/src/lexer"; [ -e crates/aion-awl/src/parser ] && ps="$ps crates/aion-awl/src/parser"; [ -e crates/aion-awl/src/emitter ] && ps="$ps crates/aion-awl/src/emitter"; ! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' $ps

gates.name: lints-pinned
argv: test "$(grep -c '^\[lints' crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml)" -eq 1 && grep -A1 '^\[lints\]$' crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml | grep -q '^workspace = true'

gates.name: split-complete
argv: test ! -f crates/aion-awl/src/emitter.rs && test -f crates/aion-awl/src/emitter/mod.rs && wc -l crates/aion-awl/src/emitter/*.rs | awk '$2!="total" && $1>500{bad=1; print "OVER 500:", $0} END{exit bad}' && ! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore' crates/aion-awl/src/emitter

lenses.name: correctness
lenses.charter: Hunt defects in the change itself: logic errors, unhandled cases, broken invariants, concurrency hazards, silent failure paths. Because this is a PURE MOVE, the highest-value defect class is a move that silently changes behavior: an `impl Emitter` method relocated into a child module that now resolves a differently-scoped free helper, a `pub(super)`/`pub(crate)` widening that accidentally changes which item a call site binds to, an item-ordering change that alters trait/method resolution, or a mem::take/capture buffer interaction (self.out / self.indent) reordered across the split. Assume the split changed emitted output somewhere and try to find the input that proves it.

lenses.name: regressions
lenses.charter: Hunt collateral damage: behaviour the diff changes that the brief did not ask to change. The emitted-Gleam byte-identity invariant is paramount — the three .gleam.golden files (hello, research_report, bounded_cycle) pin `emit(&parse(source))` byte-for-byte via crates/aion-awl/tests/emitter.rs assert_golden, and the denylist test forbids direct nondeterministic runtime APIs; any diff to those goldens or to the emitted text is a regression. Also watch for a gratuitous edit smuggled in under the split (a decomposed function, a rewritten combinator, doc churn): the brief mandates a PURE MOVE, so any non-mechanical change is out-of-contract behaviour risk. Round-trip (parse . print = id) and every diagnostic message/span must be unchanged.

lenses.name: brief_compliance
lenses.charter: Hold the diff to the brief, adversarially: every acceptance criterion either demonstrably met or a blocking finding; every scope_out boundary respected; every claimed deviation actually declared. Pass-and-still-wrong is the failure class here — do not accept a claim without evidence in the diff or gate output. Confirm specifically that R3 was satisfied by introducing NO bypass (not by 'purging' something that was never there) and that emitter/mod.rs is declarations + re-exports only.

lenses.name: spec_fidelity
lenses.charter: Read AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md Wave 0 (the AWL0-REFAC-001d entry — `emitter.rs (2043) -> emitter/`, which lists NO allow-block clause) and the committed brief docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001d-input.json, and check the implementation clause by clause against R1-R4: (R1) emitter.rs replaced by an emitter/ folder module of REAL responsibility-named children each <=500 lines, mod.rs = declarations/re-exports only, the single large Emitter impl split across modules by concern using multiple impl blocks, lib.rs untouched; (R2) zero behavior/API change — `pub use emitter::{EmitError, emit};` unchanged so aion-cli compiles UNMODIFIED, the three .gleam.golden files byte-identical and passing (the emitted Gleam is the regression invariant), round-trip intact; (R3) the emitter/ tree carries ZERO bypass attributes AND the split introduced NO lint-motivated logic edit — because authoring measurement confirmed emitter.rs had NO allow block and NO lint debt (base clippy green), there is NOTHING to purge, so verify NO gratuitous function decomposition / unwrap rewrite / doc churn was smuggled in, Cargo.toml [lints] byte-identical (workspace = true only), clippy -D warnings green; (R4) the diff touches ONLY emitter paths. include!() splices, part_NN/chunk/body.rs naming, ANY lint-policy weakening (source attrs OR manifest), any edit to lib.rs/aion-cli/printer.rs/tests/goldens, or any change outside the fence are automatic blocking findings — both prior dispatches of the parent brief were rejected for the fraud patterns. Verify child-module names honestly describe their contents and that the emitter module stays private + re-exported.
